description_long: This is a tall, narrow vase with a bulbous lower body that tapers smoothly into a short, slightly flared neck and rim. The surface is matte and textured, with a mottled brown coloration and subtle, darker streaks that give it an earthy, weathered appearance. A single, angular handle is attached to one side, extending from the upper neck to the midsection, providing both visual interest and functional grip. The body of the vase shows faint, irregular lines and shallow cracks, suggesting a hand-crafted or aged ceramic construction. The interior appears hollow, suitable for holding water or decorative items. The overall form and finish evoke a rustic, utilitarian style often found in traditional or outdoor urban settings.
description: A tall, brown, matte-finished vase with a bulbous body, flared neck, and a single angular handle, featuring subtle streaks and a weathered texture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the vase's smooth, rounded body and flared neck, with the handle not visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the prominent angular handle attached from the neck to the midsection and visible surface streaks.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the handle and the vase's gently tapering form.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the handle visible on the left, showing the vase's continuous, unbroken surface on the right.
